steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The equation $(x+64)^2 = -100$ asks how many distinct real values of $x$ satisfy it. Any real number squared is $\geq 0$, so the left side can never equal a negative number.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Exactly one": Would require one real $x$ making a square equal to $-100$. Impossible. ✗
  • Choice B — "Exactly two": A perfect-square equation like $(x+64)^2 = k$ has two solutions when $k > 0$, one when $k = 0$, and none when $k \lt 0$. Since $k = -100 \lt 0$, this is ruled out. ✗
  • Choice C — "Infinitely many": No real number satisfies the equation, let alone infinitely many. ✗
  • Choice D — "Zero": No real $x$ makes a square negative. Zero solutions. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
